mission: Publish the canonical Phase 0 runtime-topology contract as machine-readable law so workload families, trust-zone boundaries, failure domains, egress posture, and runtime publication placeholders become explicit inputs for later gateway, release, and frontend tasks.

trust_zones.purpose: TLS termination, origin allowlisting, request shaping, and zero-trust browser pre-filtering.
trust_zones.notes: This is the first browser-contacting band, but it is not the published route-compute boundary.

trust_zones.purpose: Static shell, publication bundle, and design/runtime parity delivery only.
trust_zones.notes: Shell assets stay exact to the promoted runtime bundle and may not become a hidden BFF.

trust_zones.purpose: The only browser-addressable compute boundary for published gateway surfaces.
primary_controls: No direct data-plane or adapter access
trust_zones.notes: Later seq_047 refines exact gateway splits, but seq_046 already freezes the zone and bridge law.

trust_zones.purpose: Canonical command and projection runtime planes with no direct browser reachability.
trust_zones.notes: Command and projection stay separate workload families even when they deploy from the same repository.

trust_zones.purpose: External effect dispatch, provider callback ingress, and simulator seams.
trust_zones.notes: Live-provider dispatch and simulator backplanes stay in the same trust zone but split at the workload-family level.

trust_zones.purpose: Relational, projection, queue, cache, object, and immutable store classes with explicit restore law.
primary_controls: Tenant tuple on every key or stronger scope tuple
trust_zones.notes: Queues and caches are stateful runtime concerns, not hidden details inside app or worker config.

trust_zones.purpose: Assurance slices, release/watch tuple publication, audit, resilience, and KMS-bound control paths.
trust_zones.notes: Support, operations, governance, and release controls consume one assurance plane instead of inventing local truth.

trust_zone_boundaries.notes: Static delivery remains bundle-bound and may not become a mutable runtime hop.
This is the only browser-to-compute bridge beyond the edge.
Gateway surfaces may read projections and submit commands only through declared bridge placeholders.
Support replay, operations drilldown, and governance shells may query assurance slices without bypassing the control plane.
Data-plane access is internal-only and tuple-bound.
Command handoff into integration always passes through durable queues or simulator contracts.
Mutation and projection planes both depend on exact assurance slices before live widening.
Provider outcomes settle through durable state before they can affect user-visible posture.
Integration failures widen only through explicit assurance tuples and watch publication.
Assurance is the only plane allowed to publish restore and release tuple verdicts into stateful stores.

workload_family_catalog.notes: Edge routing is shared but intentionally narrow: no PHI assembly and no direct adapter or data egress.

workload_family_catalog.split_rationale: Split from the published gateway because static publication, release freeze linkage, and no-runtime-egress posture differ materially from browser-addressable compute.
workload_family_catalog.notes: Shell delivery stays exact to the RuntimePublicationBundle and cannot hide mutation or session logic.

workload_family_catalog.split_rationale: Separated from static shell delivery because differing session policy, tenant isolation, bridge authority, and release-freeze posture would make one combined family unsafe.
workload_family_catalog.notes: This is the only family allowed to bridge browser traffic into command, projection, or assurance planes.
Canonical writes, blockers, settlements, and route-intent enforcement stay here and nowhere in the shell layer.
Projection is audience-safe derived truth only; it never upgrades canonical write authority.

workload_family_catalog.split_rationale: Split from simulator work because live-provider dispatch has materially different egress, approval, and receipt-settlement posture than the local proof twin backplane.
workload_family_catalog.notes: All live-provider motion stays in durable queues and declared effect contracts; direct browser or data-plane reach is forbidden.

workload_family_catalog.split_rationale: Non-authoritative simulator seams stay out of preprod and production because proof-twin behavior must never be misread as live provider confirmation.
workload_family_catalog.notes: The simulator backplane is allowed only in nonprod rings and only as a declared proof twin or fallback seam.
Relational, queue, object, cache, and audit stores are explicit runtime concerns with restore law, not hidden infrastructure folklore.

source_refs: blueprint/phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9F. Resilience architecture, restore orchestration, and chaos programme
workload_family_catalog.notes: Assurance, security, governance, operations, and release consume one exact tuple-publishing control plane.

environment_manifests.notes: This ring manifest freezes the runtime family set, legal trust-zone crossings, and release/publication placeholders required for later tasks.

ingress_catalog.notes: The public edge terminates browser TLS and performs origin, rate-limit, and bot policy checks.
Static shell delivery reads only from the promoted RuntimePublicationBundle and exact design/runtime parity artifacts.
Published gateway surfaces are the only browser-addressable compute bridge beyond the edge.
Provider callbacks enter only through durable callback and correlation seams in the integration perimeter.

source_refs: blueprint/phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9F. Resilience architecture, restore orchestration, and chaos programme

assurance_slices.notes: The exact topology tuple governs whether a ring may widen or fail over.
Release, operational readiness, and runtime publication must stay on one watch tuple.
Restore and failover activation require current rehearsal and store parity proof.

blocked_crossings.reason: Browser or edge traffic may not bypass published gateway surfaces into command.
Projection reads remain behind declared gateway surfaces; the edge does not query projections directly.
Edge traffic may not reach provider or callback runtimes directly.
No browser- or edge-reachable service may access raw stores directly.
Static publication delivery cannot become a shadow mutation plane.
Shell assets are bundle-bound and may not query projections directly.
Gateway surfaces may not bypass command or projection and may never reach raw stores.
Gateway surfaces may not dispatch directly to providers or callback runtimes.
Simulators are internal proof twins only and are never browser-addressable.
Operational, support, and governance browser access reaches assurance slices only through published gateway surfaces.

topology_defects.title: Published gateway surfaces still use placeholder bridge rows until seq_047 lands exact splits.
topology_defects.summary: Seq_046 freezes the gateway family and browser bridge law now, but route-family ownership and final gateway split logic remain a seq_047 deliverable.

topology_defects.title: ReleaseApprovalFreeze and channel-manifest bindings remain typed placeholders.
topology_defects.summary: Every environment manifest now carries explicit release and channel placeholder refs, but seq_050 will bind them to the frontend and publication tuples.

topology_defects.title: Integration egress allowlists are explicit but still provider-neutral at the hostname or CIDR level.
topology_defects.summary: Seq_046 freezes which families may egress and under what approval law, while later onboarding work supplies provider-specific endpoint inventories.

dependency_effect_budgets.degraded_mode_default: Preserve a read-only, pending-delivery, or repair-needed posture until current delivery evidence or a governed fallback settles the route.
dependency_effect_budgets.manual_fallback_default: Use controlled resend, callback escalation, or support repair under the same authoritative communication chain.

dependency_effect_budgets.degraded_mode_default: Freeze the route in claim-pending or auth-read-only posture until writable authority is re-proven for the same subject and route intent.
dependency_effect_budgets.manual_fallback_default: Route to support-assisted recovery or a secure-link continuation path without widening identity truth.

dependency_effect_budgets.degraded_mode_default: Keep enrichment fully off and rely on local matching only; do not widen patient identity confidence from incomplete or legally unapproved PDS signals.
dependency_effect_budgets.manual_fallback_default: Support-assisted correction or governed local review without changing the baseline identity contract.

dependency_effect_budgets.degraded_mode_default: Withdraw seeded continuation trust and fall back to challenge-based continuation or another governed contact route when recipient or delivery truth is disputed.
dependency_effect_budgets.manual_fallback_default: Support reissue or bounded callback under the same grant lineage rather than silent re-send.

assumptions.reason: No conflicting ADR pins a vendor-specific runtime IaC tool in the repository.
Task 011 froze UK region roles without binding the repo to a specific cloud SKU vocabulary.

follow_on_dependencies.scope: Object storage private endpoints and retention bucket pathing.
follow_on_dependencies.notes: par_084 publishes the data-plane network seam only; bucket resources remain owned by par_086.

follow_on_dependencies.scope: Queueing, outbox/inbox brokers, and integration dispatch private connectivity.
follow_on_dependencies.notes: par_084 preserves route domains and workload-family segmentation for these paths without provisioning the bus.

follow_on_dependencies.scope: Cache plane and live-update transport overlays.
follow_on_dependencies.notes: The stateful-data trust zone stays explicit, but cache and live channel resources remain owned by par_088.

follow_on_dependencies.scope: Secret broker, KMS custody, and concrete workload-identity credential minting.
follow_on_dependencies.notes: par_084 marks every service as workload-identity-ready; par_089 owns real credential issuance and rotation.

follow_on_dependencies.scope: Audience-specific gateway ingress attachments and public origin publication.
follow_on_dependencies.notes: par_084 provisions only the published-gateway trust boundary and browser bridge law.
